msgs.text:
Turn 4 — Stage C: turn the tame/pro‑2 comparison into one uniform boundary package The next move should not be “prove the Γₐ tame quotient,” then separately “prove the Γₐ pro‑2 quotient,” then separately “prove the local versions.” That would create four brittle case proofs. The structural object should be a single reusable boundary package: a profinite source Γ is acceptable for the later induction once it has a tame quotient, a maximal pro‑2 quotient, and a full integral unramified marking making the two compatible over ℤ₂.This is exactly where the manuscript says the full ℤ₂ marking is load-bearing: the common boundary is∂bd=Ttame×Z2Π,and the tame and pro‑2 markings cannot merely agree mod 2. The manuscript explicitly says the fibre product records equality of the full unramified coordinate and that the maps for both Γₐ and GQ2 combine to epimorphisms bΓ:Γ↠∂bd.

The target of Turn 4 is therefore:lean noncomputable def boundaryPackage_GammaA :BoundaryPackage GammaA := ...noncomputable def boundaryPackage_GQ2 :BoundaryPackage GQ2Profinite := ...noncomputable def Boundary : ProfiniteGrp :=ProfiniteGrp.pullback nu_t nu_2 theorem boundary_map_surjective {Γ : ProfiniteGrp} (B : BoundaryPackage Γ) :Function.Surjective B.toBoundary := ...Once this exists, Sections 4–10 can talk about a source Γ through BoundaryPackage Γ, rather than through special facts about Γₐ or GQ2. That is the uniform statement that closes many later cases at once.The lane remains a Lean implementation plan, not the formalization itself; the attached problem statement explicitly asks for an inline plan using the coding agent and remote Lean backend.

1. Define ℤ₂ as the finite quotient object actually needed downstream Do not start by importing a broad p-adic integer tower unless mathlib already gives exactly the profinite group API needed. The boundary only needs the full tower of cyclic quotients C2m. Define a small profinite object by inverse limit:lean namespace Q2Presentation/-- The profinite group ℤ₂ as the inverse limit of `ZMod (2^n)`. -/noncomputable def ProfiniteZ2 : ProfiniteGrp := by -- inverse limit over n ≥ 1 of additive cyclic groups ZMod (2^n)exact Z2Limit.obj def C2Pow (n : ℕ) : Type := ZMod (2^n)noncomputable def z2Projection (n : ℕ) :ProfiniteZ2 ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of (C2Pow n) := ...theorem ProfiniteZ2_ext {x y : ProfiniteZ2}(h : ∀ n, z2Projection n x = z2Projection n y) :x = y := ...end Q2Presentation This is the right abstraction because Proposition 3.14 and Section 4 need compatibility after passage to every C2m, not just mod 2. The manuscript emphasizes exactly this point.Cluster work: run ZMod/additive-group variants in parallel. The likely pain point is whether ProfiniteGrp.of expects multiplicative groups. If so, use Multiplicative (ZMod (2^n)) and define notation wrappers immediately.

2. Formalize Ttame by finite-quotient semantics Continue the finite-quotient-first architecture. Do not use an ordinary discrete PresentedGroup as the main object. Prior turns already fixed Γₐ as an inverse limit of admissible finite quotients and warned that finite exact images are the right semantics.Define the tame quotient as the inverse limit of finite marked pairs (s,t) satisfying ts=t2 and generating.lean structure TameMarkedFinite where n : ℕ hn : Fact (0 < n)groupFin : Group (Fin n)s t : Fin n generates : Subgroup.closure ({s, t} : Set (Fin n)) = ⊤ tame : rconj t s = t^2 attribute [instance] TameMarkedFinite.hn attribute [instance] TameMarkedFinite.groupFin structure TameHom (A B : TameMarkedFinite) where toMonoidHom : (Fin A.n) →* (Fin B.n)map_s : toMonoidHom A.s = B.s map_t : toMonoidHom A.t = B.t noncomputable def Ttame : ProfiniteGrp :=limit tameDiagram

The finite theorem to prove uniformly is:lean theorem finite_tame_tau_odd {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G]{s t : G} (h : rconj t s = t^2) :Odd (orderOf t) := ...theorem finite_tame_normal_two_subgroup_unramified {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G]{s t : G} (hgen : Subgroup.closure ({s, t} : Set G) = ⊤)(htame : rconj t s = t^2)(N : Subgroup G) [N.Normal] (hN2 : IsPGroup 2 N) :N ≤ Subgroup.closure ({s} : Set G) := ...theorem Ttame_maximal_pro2 :MaximalPro2Quotient Ttame ≅ ProfiniteZ2 := ...noncomputable def nu_t : Ttame ⟶ ProfiniteZ2 := ...This maps directly to Lemma 3.1, Proposition 3.2, and Lemma 3.3. The manuscript proves that finite tame quotients have odd-order inertia, identifies the common tame quotient, and shows the tame group has no nontrivial closed normal pro‑2 subgroup, making the marked wild subgroup characteristic.The proof should be factored so that later finite target arguments can reuse finite_tame_tau_odd without importing profinite limits.3. Formalize Π by finite pro‑2 quotient semantics, not free pro‑2 presentations The dangerous sub-instance is the reduction of the wild word in a finite 2-group. Do not unfold the whole word in multiple proofs. Prove one uniform specialization theorem:lean structure PiMarkedFinite where n : ℕ hn : Fact (0 < n)groupFin : Group (Fin n)isTwoGroup : IsPGroup 2 (Fin n)sigma x0 x1 : Fin n generates :Subgroup.closure ({sigma, x0, x1} : Set (Fin n)) = ⊤ rel :rconj x0 (sigma^2) * x0 * comm x1 sigma = 1 noncomputable def Pi : ProfiniteGrp :=limit piDiagram noncomputable def nu_2 : Pi ⟶ ProfiniteZ2 := ...Then prove the candidate-side pro‑2 specialization in one theorem:lean theorem admissible_twoGroup_iff_piMarked {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G] [Nonempty G](hG2 : IsPGroup 2 G) (q : Marking G) :Admissible q ↔ q Gen.tau = 1 ∧Subgroup.closure ({q Gen.sigma, q Gen.x0, q Gen.x1} : Set G) = ⊤ ∧rconj (q Gen.x0) ((q Gen.sigma)^2) *q Gen.x0 *comm (q Gen.x1) (q Gen.sigma) = 1 := ...This single theorem captures Proposition 3.10: in finite 2-group quotients, τ is forced to be trivial, the ω₂-power acts as identity, and the displayed wild relation becomes x0σ2x0[x1,σ]=1.The same theorem will also feed Lemma 8.2 later, where the scalar character group is computed from the common marked pro‑2 quotient.

4. Nielsen transform as an equivalence of finite quotient categories A better Lean formalization than manipulating closed normal closures of free pro‑2 groups is to prove that the finite quotient categories for the Demushkin presentation and Π are equivalent.

Define a Demushkin finite pro‑2 quotient category:lean structure D0MarkedFinite where n : ℕ hn : Fact (0 < n)groupFin : Group (Fin n)isTwoGroup : IsPGroup 2 (Fin n)a s y : Fin n generates :Subgroup.closure ({a, s, y} : Set (Fin n)) = ⊤ rel : a^2 * s^4 * comm s y = 1

Then implement the Nielsen transform functor:lean def D0_to_Pi_marking {G : Type*} [Group G](a s y : G) : PiTriple G :={ sigma := s x1 := y x0 := s⁻² * a⁻¹ }def Pi_to_D0_marking {G : Type*} [Group G](sigma x0 x1 : G) : D0Triple G :={ a := x0⁻¹ * sigma⁻² s := sigma y := x1 }theorem nielsen_rel_equiv {G : Type*} [Group G] {a s y : G} :a^2 * s^4 * comm s y = 1 ↔ let σ := s let x1 := y let x0 := s⁻² * a⁻¹ rconj x0 (σ^2) * x0 * comm x1 σ = 1 := ...theorem D0_Pi_index_equiv :D0MarkedFinite ≌ PiMarkedFinite := ...noncomputable def D0_iso_Pi :D0 ≅ Pi := ...The manuscript proves this Nielsen move in Proposition 3.11, showing that the Demushkin relator a2s4 [s,y] transforms to x0σ2x0[x1,σ].This is a structural pattern: all future pro‑2 quotient comparisons go through the categorical equivalence D0MarkedFinite ≌ PiMarkedFinite, not through a case-specific relator rewrite.5. Local marked Demushkin theorem: isolate the high-risk interface, then prove consequences against it The local theorem is the first real high-risk component. The manuscript says Labute’s classification identifies GQ2 (2) with the rank-three Demushkin group abstractly, but that abstract identification is insufficient because the full unramified character must be preserved. The canonical Demushkin orientation plus the unramified character supplies the needed rigidity.Do not let this block all downstream code. Create the exact interface first:lean structure MarkedDemushkinQ2 where Dloc : ProfiniteGrp nu_ur : Dloc ⟶ ProfiniteZ2 a s y : Dloc dense :topologicalClosure (Subgroup.closure ({a, s, y} : Set Dloc)) = ⊤ rel :a^2 * s^4 * comm s y = 1 mark_a : nu_ur a = (-2 : ProfiniteZ2)mark_s : nu_ur s = (1 : ProfiniteZ2)mark_y : nu_ur y = 0 isMaximalPro2 :Dloc ≅ MaximalPro2Quotient GQ2Profinite

Then state the theorem:lean theorem Q2_marked_Demushkin :Nonempty MarkedDemushkinQ2 := ...

Downstream, prove:lean noncomputable def local_pro2_marked_iso :MarkedProfiniteIso (MaximalPro2Quotient GQ2Profinite) Pi nu_ur_on_maximalPro2 nu_2 := ...The proof of Q2_marked_Demushkin should later be split exactly along the manuscript:lean theorem D0_standard_demushkin_and_orientation : ...theorem Q2_marked_abelianization_orientation_initialForm : ...theorem orientation_preserving_abelian_aut_lifts : ...theorem marked_demushkin_normalization : ...The orientation-preserving lift is not decorative. The manuscript explicitly gives a counterexample showing that an un-oriented normalization with the same abelianized relation and quadratic initial form can fail; Proposition 3.9 is the structural input that fixes it.In the skeleton branch, downstream files may import Q2_marked_Demushkin with by sorry only to validate dependencies. In main-clean, no local theorem is accepted until #print axioms Q2_marked_Demushkin has no custom axiom and no sorry.

6. Define the uniform BoundaryPackage

Now package the tame and pro‑2 data uniformly:lean structure BoundaryPackage (Γ : ProfiniteGrp) where wild : ClosedNormalSubgroup Γ wild_isPro2 : IsProPClosed 2 wild tameMap : Γ ⟶ Ttame tame_surj : Function.Surjective tameMap tame_kernel : ContinuousMonoidHom.ker tameMap = wild.toSubgroup pro2Map : Γ ⟶ Pi pro2_surj : Function.Surjective pro2Map nu : Γ ⟶ ProfiniteZ2 tame_nu :tameMap ≫ nu_t = nu pro2_nu :pro2Map ≫ nu_2 = nu wild_characteristic :wild.toSubgroup = closedTwoCore Γ

Then instantiate:lean noncomputable def boundaryPackage_GammaA :BoundaryPackage GammaA := by -- tame quotient from ΓA/WA ≅ Ttame -- pro2 quotient from admissible_twoGroup_iff_piMarked -- ν from σ ↦ 1, τ,x0,x1 ↦ 0 -- compatibility by generator extensionality...noncomputable def boundaryPackage_GQ2 :BoundaryPackage GQ2Profinite := by -- tame quotient by wild inertia -- pro2 quotient via Q2_marked_Demushkin + Nielsen transform -- ν = geometric unramified character...This is the Lean target corresponding to Proposition 3.14: for Γ in {Γₐ, GQ₂}, tame and maximal pro‑2 quotient maps can be chosen so their composites with ν_t and ν_2 are the same full unramified character.

7. Boundary object and epimorphism, proved once

Define the fibre product:lean noncomputable def Boundary : ProfiniteGrp :=ProfiniteGrp.pullback nu_t nu_2 noncomputable def BoundaryPackage.toBoundary {Γ : ProfiniteGrp} (B : BoundaryPackage Γ) :Γ ⟶ Boundary :=pullback.lift B.tameMap B.pro2Map(by -- B.tame_nu and B.pro2_nu simpa [Category.assoc] using B.tame_nu.trans B.pro2_nu.symm)

Then prove a general surjectivity theorem:lean theorem boundaryPackage_toBoundary_surjective {Γ : ProfiniteGrp} (B : BoundaryPackage Γ) :Function.Surjective B.toBoundary := ...The proof should be a reusable profinite Goursat lemma, not a Γ-specific argument:lean theorem profinite_goursat_pullback_surjective {Γ A B Z : ProfiniteGrp}(fA : Γ ⟶ A) (fB : Γ ⟶ B)(νA : A ⟶ Z) (νB : B ⟶ Z)(hfA : Function.Surjective fA)(hfB : Function.Surjective fB)(hcompat : fA ≫ νA = fB ≫ νB)(hBpro2 : IsProP 2 B)(hAmax : MaximalPro2Quotient A ≅ Z)(hνAmax : νA realizes that maximal quotient) :Function.Surjective (pullback.lift fA fB hcompat) := ...The manuscript uses exactly this Goursat reasoning: any common quotient of Ttame and the pro‑2 group Π is a pro‑2 quotient of Ttame, hence comes through the full ℤ₂ quotient, and Proposition 3.14 ensures the full quotient occurs.8. Boundary-framed targets should be defined now, but not proved by induction yet Create the data structure in Stage C, while postponing the Section 8–9 recursion.lean structure BoundaryFramedTarget where Y : Type*groupY : Group Y finiteY : Fintype Y

LY : Subgroup Y LY_normal : LY.Normal LY_isTwoGroup : IsPGroup 2 LY

H : Type*groupH : Group H finiteH : Fintype H

E : Type*addCommGroupE : AddCommGroup E moduleE : Module (ZMod 2) E finiteE : Fintype E piY : Y →* H piY_surj : Function.Surjective piY ker_piY : piY.ker = LY thetaY : Y →* Multiplicative E

Then define the boundary condition generically:lean structure BoundaryFrame (Y : BoundaryFramedTarget) where alpha : Ttame ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of Y.H alpha_surj : Function.Surjective alpha psi : Pi ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of (Multiplicative Y.E)beta : Boundary ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of (Y.H × Multiplicative Y.E)beta_def : beta = boundaryBeta alpha psi

For a source Γ with a boundary package:lean def boundaryFramedSurj {Γ : ProfiniteGrp} (B : BoundaryPackage Γ)(Y : BoundaryFramedTarget) (F : BoundaryFrame Y) :={ f : SurjContHom Γ (ProfiniteGrp.of Y.Y) //qYMap Y ∘ f.hom = F.beta ∘ B.toBoundary }This reflects Definition 4.1, where Y=(Y,LY,πY,θY), LY is a finite 2-group, πY has kernel LY, and the count fixes both the tame quotient and the elementary decoration.Do not start proving Theorem 4.2 yet. The manuscript’s proof of Theorem 4.2 depends on the later source interface, exact-image recursion, Fourier inversion, and strict decrease of marked 2-kernels.

9. Exact-image inheritance lemma for boundary-framed targets This is the structural lemma that prevents later case-hammering:lean def exactImageTarget {Γ : ProfiniteGrp} (B : BoundaryPackage Γ)(Y : BoundaryFramedTarget) (F : BoundaryFrame Y)(f : Γ ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of Y.Y)(hf : qYMap Y ∘ f = F.beta ∘ B.toBoundary) :BoundaryFramedTarget := ...theorem exactImageTarget_lt_or_eq {Γ : ProfiniteGrp} (B : BoundaryPackage Γ)(Y : BoundaryFramedTarget) (F : BoundaryFrame Y)(f : Γ ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of Y.Y)(hf : qYMap Y ∘ f = F.beta ∘ B.toBoundary) :-- exact image inherits `LY ∩ image`, `πY|`, `θY|` True := ...The manuscript explicitly says exact-image strata inherit the same boundary-framed structure when J≤Y projects onto H, and this is why later partitioning by image subgroup stays within the same category.This lemma should be finished before any lifting theory. It will be used in Sections 8–9 every time a proper image or central-cover image appears.

10. File layout for Turn 4

Use small, separately compiling files:Q2Presentation/Boundary/Z2Limit.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/TameFinite.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/Ttame.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/MaximalPro2.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/PiFinite.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/D0Finite.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/Nielsen.lean Q2Presentation/Local/MarkedDemushkinInterface.lean Q2Presentation/Local/MarkedDemushkinQ2.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/Package.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/Pullback.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/FramedTarget.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/ExactImageInherits.lean

Dependency order:

Z2Limit↓ TameFinite → Ttame → Ttame_maximal_pro2↓ PiFinite ← D0Finite ← Nielsen↓ MarkedDemushkinInterface → MarkedDemushkinQ2 → local_pro2_marked_iso↓ BoundaryPackage ΓA / GQ2↓ Boundary pullback and boundary map surjectivity↓ BoundaryFramedTarget and exact-image inheritance 11. Coding-agent work orders Give the GPT-5.5-Pro coding agent narrow tasks.C1 — Z2Limit.lean Implement ProfiniteZ2 as an inverse limit of ZMod (2^n) or Multiplicative (ZMod (2^n)). Prove projection extensionality and define the elements 0, 1, and -2 used in marked statements. Return only Lean code.C2 — TameFinite.lean Prove finite_tame_tau_odd, the finite classification enough to show that the maximal pro‑2 quotient of Ttame is ProfiniteZ2, and the no-normal-pro‑2-in-tame-kernel theorem. Do not import Γₐ or Galois theory.C3 — PiFinite.lean Given Stage A’s word evaluator, prove the uniform finite 2-group specialization of the Q₂ words. The agent may unfold Q2Word only in this file. All later files must use the theorem admissible_twoGroup_iff_piMarked.C4 — Nielsen.lean Implement D0MarkedFinite, PiMarkedFinite, and the finite quotient category equivalence induced by the Nielsen transform. Prove the relation equivalence for arbitrary groups first, then transport it to the finite pro‑2 objects.C5 — MarkedDemushkinInterface.lean State the exact local theorem interface and all downstream consequences. Do not attempt local reciprocity or Labute inside this file. The interface must contain the full νur values (−2,1,0), not only mod‑2 values.C6 — Package.lean Define BoundaryPackage, instantiate it for Γₐ from the finite quotient semantics, instantiate it for GQ2 from the marked Demushkin interface, and prove the compatibility equations by generator/dense-generation extensionality.C7 — Pullback.lean Prove the reusable profinite Goursat/pullback surjectivity lemma. Then derive boundaryPackage_toBoundary_surjective.C8 — FramedTarget.lean and ExactImageInherits.lean Define boundary-framed targets and exact-image inheritance. Do not prove the Section 8 recursion yet.

12. 100-core compile matrix for this turn

Use the remote backend as a theorem-selection engine:15 jobs Z2Limit variants: additive vs multiplicative, index by ℕ vs positive ℕ 15 jobs finite tame lemmas: orderOf, oddness, normal 2-subgroup, maximal pro2 quotient 15 jobs Ttame inverse-limit construction and map to ProfiniteZ2 15 jobs finite 2-group specialization of the Q₂ word into Π 10 jobs D0/Pi Nielsen relation equivalence and category equivalence 10 jobs BoundaryPackage wiring and generator extensionality 10 jobs profinite Goursat / pullback surjectivity 5 jobs boundary-framed target exact-image inheritance 5 jobs import minimization and `#print axioms` sweeps

Every job should emit:lean

#print axioms theorem_name The main-clean branch accepts only the finite-algebraic and profinite-package pieces that are sorry-free. The local marked Demushkin interface may exist with sorry only in skeleton, while the coding agent and cluster continue to work on its proof. The previous plan already requires no hidden computational verification and no custom mathematical axiom in the final theorem.

13. Turn 4 completion criterion

Stage C is stable when these compile:lean

#check Q2Presentation.ProfiniteZ2

#check Q2Presentation.Ttame

#check Q2Presentation.nu_t

#check Q2Presentation.finite_tame_tau_odd

#check Q2Presentation.Ttame_maximal_pro2

#check Q2Presentation.Pi

#check Q2Presentation.nu_2

#check Q2Presentation.admissible_twoGroup_iff_piMarked

#check Q2Presentation.D0_Pi_index_equiv

#check Q2Presentation.D0_iso_Pi

#check Q2Presentation.Q2_marked_Demushkin

#check Q2Presentation.local_pro2_marked_iso

#check Q2Presentation.BoundaryPackage

#check Q2Presentation.boundaryPackage_GammaA

#check Q2Presentation.boundaryPackage_GQ2

#check Q2Presentation.Boundary

#check Q2Presentation.boundaryPackage_toBoundary_surjective

#check Q2Presentation.BoundaryFramedTarget

#check Q2Presentation.exactImageTarget At that point the formalization has crossed the first conceptual boundary: Γₐ and GQ2 are no longer just two profinite groups with matching finite quotient counts waiting to be proved; they are two sources equipped with the same fully marked tame–pro‑2 boundary. That is exactly the interface needed before the finite lifting theory of Sections 5–6 and the exact-image recursion of Sections 7–9.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 4 turns Stage C into a uniform Lean boundary package: Ttame, Π, full ℤ₂ markings, marked local Demushkin normalization, the fibre product Boundary, source maps bΓ, and exact-image inheritance for boundary-framed targets.

GAP: The high-risk local theorem Q2_marked_Demushkin is still only an interface until Labute classification, local reciprocity, orientation-preserving automorphism lifting, and the full unramified marking are formalized without custom axioms.

NEXT: Run the Stage C compile matrix, promote the finite tame/pro‑2/Nielsen/boundary-package files to main-clean, and keep the local marked Demushkin proof in a separate long-running theorem track while Stage E signatures are drafted against BoundaryPackage.[tag: STAGE-C-BOUNDARY-PACKAGE]
